{{Short description|Film award ceremony in 2005}} {{Use mdy dates|date=February 2025}} {{Infobox film awards | number = 62 | award = Golden Globe Awards | image = | caption = | date = January 16, 2005 |site=[[Beverly Hilton Hotel]]
[[Beverly Hills]], [[Los Angeles]], [[California]]| best_film_drama = ''[[The Aviator (2004 film)|The Aviator]]'' | best_film_musical_or_comedy = ''[[Sideways]]'' | best_drama_series = ''[[Nip/Tuck]]'' | best_musical_or_comedy_series = ''[[Desperate Housewives]]'' | best_miniseries_or_tv_movie = ''[[The Life and Death of Peter Sellers]]'' |most_wins=(3) ''The Aviator''|most_nominations=(7) ''Sideways''| last = 61st | next = 63rd | network = [[NBC]] }} The '''62nd [[Golden Globe Award]]s''', honoring the best in film and television for 2004, were held on January 16, 2005.{{cite news|last1=LaSalle|first1=Mick|title='Sideways' wins expected Golden Globe; 'Aviator' success makes Oscar handicappers scratch heads|url=http://www.sfgate.com/entertainment/article/Sideways-wins-expected-Golden-Globe-Aviator-2704556.php|access-date=10 January 2018|work=[[San Francisco Chronicle]]|date=17 January 2005}}{{cite news|last1=Welkos|first1=Robert|last2=King|first2=Susan|title=A Golden glow|url=https://www.latimes.com/archives/la-xpm-2005-jan-17-et-ggmain17-story.html|access-date=10 January 2018|work=Los Angeles Times|date=17 January 2005}}{{cite news|title=DiCaprio, Foxx, Swank, Bening Grab Golden Globes|url=https://www.npr.org/templates/story/story.php?storyId=4455685|access-date=10 January 2018|work=NPR|date=17 January 2005}}{{cite web|last1=Karger|first1=Dave|last2=Cruz|first2=Clarissa|last3=Weiner|first3=Allison|title=EW recaps the Golden Globes|url=https://ew.com/article/2005/01/24/ew-recaps-golden-globes/|website=Entertainment Weekly|access-date=10 January 2018|date=24 January 2005}} The nominations were announced on December 13, 2004.{{cite news|last1=Snyder|first1=Gabriel|title=Globes noms all about booze and Hughes|url=https://variety.com/2004/film/awards/globes-noms-all-about-booze-and-hughes-1117914901/|access-date=10 January 2018|work=Variety|date=13 December 2004}}{{cite news|title=Aviator soars in Golden Globe nominations|url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2004/dec/13/awardsandprizes.news|access-date=10 January 2018|work=The Guardian|date=13 December 2004}}{{cite news|title=2004 Golden Globe Awards nominees|url=https://usatoday30.usatoday.com/life/movies/movieawards/goldenglobes/2004-12-13-nominee-list_x.htm|access-date=10 January 2018|work=USA Today|date=13 December 2004}} ''[[Sideways]]'' received the most nominations (7). ''[[The Aviator (2004 film)|The Aviator]]'' won the most awards, with 3 (including Best Motion Picture – Drama). ''[[Finding Neverland (film)|Finding Neverland]]'' had the most nominations (5) without a single win. ==Winners and nominees== [[File:LeonardoDiCaprioNov08.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Leonardo DiCaprio]], Best Actor in a Motion Picture – Drama winner]] [[File:Life Ball 2013 - magenta carpet Hilary Swank 02.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Hilary Swank]], Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Drama winner]] [[File:Jamie Foxx by Gage Skidmore.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Jamie Foxx]], Best Actor in a Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy winner]][[File:AnnetteBeningSept2013TIFF.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Annette Bening]], Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy winner]] [[File:Clive Owen (Berlin Film Festival 2009).jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Clive Owen]], Best Supporting Actor winner]] [[File:Natalie Portman at TIFF 2009.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Natalie Portman]], Best Supporting Actress winner]] [[File:IanMcShane06.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Ian McShane]], Best Actor in a Television Series – Drama winner]] [[File:Mariska Hargitay 2011.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Mariska Hargitay]], Best Actress in a Television Series – Drama winner]] [[File:JasonBateman-LF-01.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Jason Bateman]], Best Actor in a Television Series – Musical or Comedy winner]] [[File:Teri Hatcher- World of Color Premiere 21.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Teri Hatcher]], Best Actress in a Television Series – Musical or Comedy winner]] [[File:Geoffrey Rush Cannes 2011.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Geoffrey Rush]], Best Actor in a Miniseries or Television Film winner]] [[File:Glenn Close (11442234855).jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Glenn Close]], Best Actress in a Miniseries or Television Film winner]] [[File:William Shatner by Gage Skidmore 2.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[William Shatner]], Best Supporting Actor in a Series, Miniseries, or Television Film winner]] [[File:Anjelica Huston 2 Met Opera 2010 Shankbone.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Anjelica Huston]], Best Supporting Actress in a Series, Miniseries, or Television Film winner]] These are the nominees for the 62nd Golden Globe Awards.
